enrichment.short_description: Publii is a free, open-source desktop CMS for building and publishing static websites without server-side hosting requirements.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Free, open-source desktop CMS (GPL-3.0) for building static websites, with deployment to GitHub Pages, Netlify, S3, and more.

features.title: Multiple post editors
features.description: Includes image galleries, video support, and code syntax highlighting for writing posts.

features.title: Built-in performance tools
features.description: Automatic HTML/CSS compression, lazy-loaded responsive images, and WebP conversion.

features.title: GDPR/CCPA compliance tools
features.description: Advanced cookie banner and Google Consent Mode v2 support built into the app.

features.title: SEO structured data
features.description: Structured data markup, Open Graph tags, and auto-generated XML sitemaps.

features.title: Flexible deployment
features.description: Publish directly to GitHub Pages, GitLab, Netlify, Amazon S3, Google Cloud, or SFTP/FTPS.

features.title: Cross-platform desktop app
features.description: Runs natively on Windows 10+, macOS 12+, and 64-bit Linux.

pros: Free and open source under the GPL-3.0 license
Generates static sites, so there's no server-side hosting to maintain or secure
Wide range of built-in deployment targets (GitHub Pages, Netlify, S3, etc.)
Built-in SEO and GDPR/CCPA compliance tooling
Works fully offline as a local desktop app
cons: Desktop-only workflow with no browser-based editing
Publishing requires a manual build-and-deploy step from the app
Premium themes and plugins in the marketplace cost extra
No built-in multi-user/team collaboration

faqs.question: Is Publii free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core Publii desktop app is free and open source under the GPL-3.0 license. Optional premium themes and plugins are sold separately in its marketplace.

faqs.question: What platforms does Publii run on?
faqs.answer: Publii is a desktop application available for Windows 10+, macOS 12+, and 64-bit Linux.

faqs.question: Where can I host a site built with Publii?
faqs.answer: Publii can deploy to GitHub Pages, GitLab, Netlify, Amazon S3, Google Cloud, SFTP/FTPS, or manually export files.

faqs.question: Is Publii open source?
faqs.answer: Yes. Publii is built with Electron and Vue.js and its source code is available on GitHub under the GPL-3.0 license.

use_cases.title: Static blogging without a server
use_cases.description: Write and publish posts locally, then deploy a static site with no backend to maintain.

use_cases.title: Privacy-first business websites
use_cases.description: Use built-in GDPR/CCPA and cookie-consent tooling to publish compliant marketing sites.

use_cases.title: Free static hosting workflows
use_cases.description: Deploy directly to free static hosts like GitHub Pages or Netlify from the desktop app.

features: Deployment to GitHub Pages, Netlify, S3, and more

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Publii?
paragraphs: Publii is a free, open-source static site CMS delivered as a desktop application for Windows, macOS, and Linux. Rather than running on a web server, Publii lets you write and manage content locally, then build and publish a static website to a host of your choosing.

The app includes built-in tools for SEO (structured data, Open Graph, XML sitemaps) and privacy compliance (GDPR/CCPA cookie banners), along with support for deploying to GitHub Pages, GitLab, Netlify, Amazon S3, Google Cloud, or SFTP/FTPS.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Publii is for
paragraphs: Publii suits bloggers, writers, and small businesses who want a traditional CMS-style editing experience without paying for server hosting or managing site security. It also appeals to developers who want a free, open-source way to generate and deploy static sites to platforms like GitHub Pages or Netlify.

enrichment.short_description: Micro.blog is a blogging and podcasting platform starting at $5/month for hosting at your own custom domain.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Micro.blog offers blog and podcast hosting from $5/month, with cross-posting to Mastodon, Threads, and Bluesky.

features.title: Custom domain hosting
features.description: Host short posts, essays, photo blogs, or newsletters at your own domain name.

features.title: Automatic cross-posting
features.description: Cross-post content to Mastodon, Threads, and Bluesky from the base plan.

features.title: Fediverse and IndieWeb support
features.description: Built-in compatibility with fediverse protocols and IndieWeb standards.

features.title: Email newsletters (Premium)
features.description: Send email newsletters and archive bookmarks or highlighted web pages on the Premium plan.

features.title: Multiple blogs (Premium)
features.description: Premium plan supports up to 5 blogs per account.

features.title: Family collaboration (Family plan)
features.description: Up to 5 members can write or edit posts across any blog on the account.

features.title: Video and podcast hosting (Studio)
features.description: Studio plan adds video hosting up to 20 minutes, podcast recording, and multi-resolution video with cross-posting to Bluesky.

pros: Publishing to your own custom domain from $5/month
Built-in cross-posting to Mastodon, Threads, and Bluesky
Fediverse and IndieWeb protocol support
Tiered plans for families and podcasters, not just single bloggers
Founded in 2017 with an established, focused product
cons: No free plan advertised
Multiple blogs, newsletters, and video hosting require upgrading past the base $5/month tier
Top Studio tier costs $20/month for full video/podcast features

faqs.question: How much does Micro.blog cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $5/month for the base Micro.blog plan, with Premium at $10/month, Family at $15/month, and Studio at $20/month.

faqs.question: What's included in the Premium plan?
faqs.answer: Premium adds support for up to 5 blogs, email newsletters, bookmark archiving, and web page highlighting.

faqs.question: Does Micro.blog support podcasts and video?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Studio plan adds video hosting up to 20 minutes, podcast recording, multiple resolutions, and video cross-posting to Bluesky.

faqs.question: Who founded Micro.blog?
faqs.answer: Micro.blog was founded in 2017 by Manton Reece.

use_cases.title: Independent microblogging
use_cases.description: Publish short posts and essays to your own domain with fediverse cross-posting.

use_cases.title: Family or small-team blogging
use_cases.description: Use the Family plan to let up to 5 people write or edit posts across shared blogs.

use_cases.title: Podcasting and video publishing
use_cases.description: Record and host podcasts and short videos using the Studio plan.

features: Blog hosting at your own domain
Up to 5 members can write/edit posts

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Micro.blog?
paragraphs: Micro.blog is a blogging platform, founded in 2017 by Manton Reece, that lets users publish short posts, essays, photo blogs, and email newsletters at their own domain name. It integrates with the fediverse and IndieWeb standards and automatically cross-posts content to networks like Mastodon, Threads, and Bluesky.

Pricing starts at $5/month for the base plan and scales up through Premium ($10/month), Family ($15/month), and Studio ($20/month), each adding capabilities such as multiple blogs, newsletters, multi-user collaboration, and podcast/video hosting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Micro.blog is for
paragraphs: Micro.blog fits independent writers who want to own their content at a custom domain while staying connected to the fediverse, as well as families or small groups who want to co-manage blogs, and podcasters who need built-in recording and hosting through the Studio plan.

enrichment.short_description: Blot turns a synced folder into a website for $6/month per site, with hosting and templates included.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Blot is a $6/month hosted blogging tool that publishes a folder of files as a website, no CMS required.

features.title: Folder-to-website publishing
features.description: Turns a folder of files into a live website rather than requiring a web-based CMS.

features.title: Drag-and-drop publishing
features.description: Add or edit content by dragging files into your synced folder.

features.title: Editor integration
features.description: Designed to work alongside existing text editors you already use.

features.title: All templates included
features.description: Every plan includes access to all available site templates.

features.title: Custom domains and SSL
features.description: Custom domain support and SSL certificates are included in the price.

pros: Simple, file-based publishing workflow with no CMS interface to learn
All templates included at a single flat price
Custom domain and SSL support included
Over a decade of operating history
Annual billing available for a discount on the base monthly rate
cons: No free trial
Single flat pricing plan offers less flexibility than tiered platforms
Priced per site, so multi-site use adds up
No dedicated mobile or desktop apps mentioned

faqs.question: How much does Blot cost?
faqs.answer: Blot costs $6 per month per site, billed monthly by default with an annual billing option available after signup.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: No free trial is advertised on Blot's pricing page.

faqs.question: What's included in the price?
faqs.answer: Hosting, all templates, custom domain support, SSL certificates, and customer support are included.

faqs.question: How does Blot publishing work?
faqs.answer: Blot turns a folder of files into a website — you add, edit, or delete files and Blot publishes the changes.

faqs.question: What payment methods does Blot accept?
faqs.answer: Blot processes payments through Stripe and PayPal, and subscriptions can be canceled anytime.

use_cases.title: Folder-based blogging
use_cases.description: Publish a website directly from a folder of files using your existing text editor.

use_cases.title: Migrating existing notes online
use_cases.description: Turn an existing folder of documents or notes into a public site.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Blot?
paragraphs: Blot is a hosted publishing tool that turns a folder of files into a website. Instead of writing posts inside a web-based CMS, users add or edit files in a folder using their own text editor, and Blot publishes the changes automatically.

The service costs $6 per month per site, with that price covering hosting, all available templates, custom domain support, and SSL certificates. Blot has operated for over a decade and offers documentation, example sites, and developer resources.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Blot is for
paragraphs: Blot is aimed at writers and developers who'd rather work in their existing text editor and file system than a browser-based CMS, including people looking to publish an existing folder of notes or documents as a public website.

enrichment.short_description: Bear Blog is a free, open-source blogging platform with no trackers, ads, or JavaScript, and ~2.7kb pages.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Bear Blog is a free, privacy-first blogging platform with zero trackers, ads, or JavaScript and tiny page sizes.

features.title: Zero trackers, ads, or JavaScript
features.description: Pages are built without client-side scripts, ad code, or analytics trackers.

features.title: Extremely lightweight pages
features.description: Average page size is approximately 2.7kb.

features.title: Custom domain support
features.description: Blogs can be published under a custom domain.

features.title: RSS and Atom feeds
features.description: Built-in feed support for readers and aggregators.

features.title: Free themes
features.description: A selection of free themes is available out of the box.

features.title: Open-source codebase
features.description: Source code is publicly available on GitHub (HermanMartinus/bearblog).

pros: Free to use with no advertised paid tier
Open source, with the code available on GitHub
No trackers, ads, or JavaScript, supporting a strong privacy stance
Very small average page size (~2.7kb) for fast loading
Custom domain support included
cons: Deliberately minimal feature set compared to full-featured CMS platforms
No stated support tiers or SLAs
Design customization is limited by the platform's no-bloat philosophy

faqs.question: Is Bear Blog free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bear Blog is free to use.

faqs.question: Is Bear Blog open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, its source code is publicly available on GitHub at HermanMartinus/bearblog.

faqs.question: Does Bear Blog use JavaScript or trackers?
faqs.answer: No. Bear Blog is built with no JavaScript, ads, or trackers by design.

faqs.question: Can I use a custom domain with Bear Blog?
faqs.answer: Yes, custom domain support is included.

use_cases.title: Distraction-free writing
use_cases.description: Publish text-focused posts on a fast, minimal platform without extra bloat.
use_cases.audience: Writers who prioritize content over design

use_cases.title: Privacy-conscious blogging
use_cases.description: Run a blog with no trackers, ads, or client-side scripts.

use_cases.title: Self-hostable, inspectable blogging
use_cases.description: Review or adapt the open-source codebase on GitHub.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Bear Blog?
paragraphs: Bear Blog is a free, open-source blogging platform built around simplicity and speed. Pages contain no trackers, ads, or JavaScript, and average roughly 2.7kb in size, with the project's source code publicly available on GitHub.
It includes custom domain support, RSS and Atom feeds, and a set of free themes, positioning itself against feature-heavy, script-laden blogging platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Bear Blog is for
paragraphs: Bear Blog suits writers who want a fast, no-frills place to publish text-first content, along with privacy-conscious users who want to avoid trackers and ads, and developers interested in an open-source blogging codebase.

name: Plume
skip_reason: Official site (joinplu.me) states the project is not actively maintained ('Plume developers have less time and Plume is not actively maintained') and directs users to alternatives such as WriteFreely and WordPress with ActivityPub plugins. This meets the abandoned/discontinued exclusion criteria.

enrichment.short_description: Shopware is a German ecommerce platform with a free open-source Community Edition and paid plans from around €600/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Shopware offers a free MIT-licensed Community Edition plus paid SaaS/PaaS ecommerce plans starting around €600/month.

features.title: Shopware Intelligence Copilot
features.description: AI-assisted commerce management built into the platform.

features.title: Integrated CMS, SEO, and analytics
features.description: Built-in content management, search visibility, and data-analysis tools.

features.title: Flow Builder and Rule Builder
features.description: Visual tools for automation and custom business logic.

features.title: B2B components
features.description: Enterprise-level features for business-to-business selling.

features.title: Omnichannel connect
features.description: Multi-channel sales capabilities across storefronts and channels.

features.title: Digital sales rooms
features.description: Specialized B2B sales environments available on higher tiers.

features.title: Shopware Payments
features.description: Integrated payment processing within the platform.

pros: Free, open-source Community Edition under the MIT license
Flexible deployment options: self-hosted, SaaS, or PaaS
Large partner ecosystem with 1,200+ certified agencies
Strong B2B feature set alongside B2C capabilities
Named a Visionary in the 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Digital Commerce
cons: Top-tier Beyond plan requires a custom sales quote
Pricing is tied to gross merchandise value (GMV) and other business factors, not fully transparent
Evolve and Beyond tiers are enterprise-priced (€2,400+/month)
Full AI feature set (Shopware Intelligence+) costs extra (€19–€29/month)

faqs.question: Is Shopware free?
faqs.answer: Shopware Community Edition is free and open source under the MIT license. Paid Rise, Evolve, and Beyond plans add cloud hosting and additional features.

faqs.question: How much does Shopware cost?
faqs.answer: Rise starts from €600/month, Evolve from €2,400/month (both excl. VAT), and Beyond is custom-priced.

faqs.question: Is Shopware open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Edition is open source and MIT licensed.

faqs.question: Where is Shopware based?
faqs.answer: Shopware is headquartered in Germany.

use_cases.title: Enterprise omnichannel commerce
use_cases.description: Run B2C and B2B storefronts across multiple sales channels with built-in CMS and AI tools.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted open-source ecommerce
use_cases.description: Deploy the free Community Edition and manage infrastructure independently.

use_cases.title: B2B wholesale commerce
use_cases.description: Use B2B components and digital sales rooms for wholesale and enterprise buying.

features: Enhanced support (4-hour response time, phone support)
24/7 priority support with dedicated account manager

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Shopware?
paragraphs: Shopware is a German ecommerce platform offering a free, open-source Community Edition (MIT licensed) alongside paid SaaS and PaaS plans. It includes an integrated CMS, SEO and analytics tools, a Flow Builder for automation, and B2B-specific features such as digital sales rooms and dynamic access rules.

Paid plans start with Rise from €600/month, scale to Evolve from €2,400/month, and top out with the custom-priced Beyond tier, each adding support levels, B2B capabilities, and access to features like the Shopware Intelligence Copilot.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Shopware is for
paragraphs: Shopware serves both self-hosting merchants using the free Community Edition and enterprise brands needing managed cloud hosting, B2B wholesale functionality, and omnichannel selling, supported by a network of 1,200+ certified partner agencies.

enrichment.short_description: Sylius is an open-source, MIT-licensed headless ecommerce platform built on Symfony and PHP, based in Opole, Poland.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Sylius is a free, open-source headless ecommerce platform for PHP/Symfony developers, with a paid Sylius Plus tier.

features.title: REST APIs via API Platform
features.description: Powerful REST APIs built on API Platform for headless commerce implementations.

features.title: B2C and B2B support
features.description: Designed to support both business-to-consumer and business-to-business commerce models.

features.title: Docker and Kubernetes-ready
features.description: Scalable architecture that supports containerized deployment.

features.title: Behavior-Driven Development testing
features.description: Built with a BDD testing approach for reliability.

features.title: Modular, customizable architecture
features.description: Core platform designed to be extended and customized for bespoke commerce needs.

features.title: Multi-vendor marketplace (Dafre)
features.description: Dedicated product for building multi-vendor marketplaces.

features.title: B2B setup (Elesto)
features.description: Dedicated product offering B2B-specific setup options.

pros: Free, open-source Community Edition under the MIT license
Strong developer and testing culture with Behavior-Driven Development
Large community: 7,400+ developers on Slack and 700+ contributors
Used by major brands including Mytheresa, Carrefour, and Jägermeister
Headless, API-first architecture built on Symfony
cons: Requires PHP/Symfony expertise; not a no-code platform
Sylius Plus pricing is not publicly disclosed
Best suited to mid-market/enterprise needs rather than very small stores

faqs.question: Is Sylius free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Sylius Community Edition is free and open source under the MIT license.

faqs.question: What is Sylius Plus?
faqs.answer: Sylius Plus is the commercial edition, offering support and advanced features on top of the Community Edition.

faqs.question: What technology does Sylius use?
faqs.answer: Sylius is built on PHP and the Symfony framework, using API Platform for its REST APIs.

faqs.question: Where is Sylius based?
faqs.answer: Sylius is headquartered in Opole, Poland.

use_cases.title: Custom headless commerce
use_cases.description: Build bespoke B2C or B2B storefronts using Sylius's REST API and modular architecture.

use_cases.title: Symfony-based commerce development
use_cases.description: Use an ecommerce framework built for PHP/Symfony teams with a BDD testing culture.

use_cases.title: Marketplace and B2B commerce
use_cases.description: Build multi-vendor marketplaces (Dafre) or B2B storefronts (Elesto) on top of Sylius.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Sylius?
paragraphs: Sylius is an open-source, headless ecommerce platform built on PHP and Symfony, using API Platform to expose REST APIs for custom commerce implementations. It's designed for mid-market and enterprise brands that need bespoke solutions rather than an out-of-the-box storefront.

The Community Edition is free and MIT licensed, while Sylius Plus adds commercial support and advanced features. The company behind Sylius is headquartered in Opole, Poland, and its ecosystem includes over 700 contributors and 7,400+ developers on Slack.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Sylius is for
paragraphs: Sylius fits development teams and CTOs who need a customizable, API-first ecommerce framework built on familiar PHP/Symfony tooling, including companies building multi-vendor marketplaces or B2B storefronts on top of the core platform.

enrichment.short_description: Medusa is an open-source, MIT-licensed commerce platform with 35k+ GitHub stars and Medusa Cloud from $29/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Medusa is an open-source (MIT) commerce platform for developers, with a managed Medusa Cloud plan starting at $29/month.

features.title: Advanced product management
features.description: Rich product pages, bundles, and bulk editing tools.

features.title: Multiple sales channels
features.description: Support for multi-store, point-of-sale, and app-based sales channels with channel-specific rules.

features.title: Inventory and multi-warehousing
features.description: Order reservations and synchronization across multiple warehouses.

features.title: Multi-regional support
features.description: Multiple currencies, local tax rules, and region-specific shipping and payments.

features.title: Promotion engine
features.description: Advanced campaign rules, customer-specific pricing, and discounts.

features.title: Customizable admin dashboard
features.description: An extensible dashboard for managing commerce operations.

features.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) support
features.description: Built-in support for AI/agent integrations via MCP, plus a Cloud CLI and Development Agent.

pros: Fully open source under the MIT license, with 35k+ GitHub stars
Medusa Cloud has no GMV-based fees — pay only for infrastructure usage
Modular architecture for custom commerce logic
Built-in multi-regional and multi-currency support
AI/agent-oriented tooling via Model Context Protocol support
cons: Developer-oriented setup requiring technical implementation
Medusa Cloud pricing scales with infrastructure usage rather than a flat rate
AI/agent features may require additional configuration

faqs.question: Is Medusa free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core Medusa platform is open source and free under the MIT license.

faqs.question: How much does Medusa Cloud cost?
faqs.answer: Medusa Cloud starts at $29/month with no GMV tax or hidden license fees; you pay for infrastructure usage.

faqs.question: What license does Medusa use?
faqs.answer: Medusa is licensed under MIT.

faqs.question: Does Medusa support AI agents?
faqs.answer: Yes, Medusa supports the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for AI and agent-driven development, along with a Development Agent and Cloud CLI tools.

use_cases.title: Custom commerce backends
use_cases.description: Build B2C or B2B commerce logic using Medusa's modular open-source framework.

use_cases.title: Open-source alternative to SaaS commerce
use_cases.description: Self-host the free core platform instead of a proprietary SaaS commerce system.

use_cases.title: AI/agent-driven commerce
use_cases.description: Use MCP support and agentic tooling to build AI-assisted commerce experiences.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Medusa?
paragraphs: Medusa is an open-source commerce platform, MIT licensed and with 35k+ GitHub stars, built for developers to create customized B2C, B2B, and marketplace commerce solutions. It includes modules for product management, multi-warehouse inventory, multi-regional pricing, and a promotion engine.

Beyond the free self-hosted core, Medusa Cloud offers managed infrastructure starting at $29/month with no GMV-based fees, along with AI and agent tooling including Model Context Protocol (MCP) support.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Medusa is for
paragraphs: Medusa is aimed at developers and technical teams who want an open-source, customizable commerce backend instead of a closed SaaS platform, including teams building AI-agent-driven commerce experiences using Medusa's MCP support.

enrichment.short_description: Saleor is an open-source, BSD-licensed headless commerce API with Saleor Cloud plans starting at $1,599/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Saleor is a GraphQL-native, open-source commerce API (BSD-3-Clause) with Saleor Cloud plans from $1,599/month.

features.title: GraphQL-native API
features.description: Native GraphQL API with 160+ webhooks for integrations.

features.title: Extensible admin dashboard
features.description: Administration dashboard with 45+ mount points for custom UI.

features.title: Dynamic product modeling
features.description: Products and data structures can be adapted on the fly.

features.title: Omnichannel processing
features.description: Supports web, mobile, retail, and agentic sales channels.

features.title: OpenTelemetry observability
features.description: Distributed system monitoring using the OpenTelemetry standard.

features.title: Configurator CLI
features.description: Automates configuration and syncs between the API and a repository.

pros: Open-source core under the BSD-3-Clause license, with 23.1k+ GitHub stars
GraphQL-native, API-first architecture
SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, and PCI-DSS compliant
Proven at scale: reported 1B+ requests and 400k orders processed monthly
Tech-agnostic integration via APIs and webhooks
cons: Saleor Cloud starts at $1,599/month for the Select tier
Overage fees (0.2%–0.8%) apply beyond each plan's GMV limit
Requires engineering resources; not a no-code/out-of-the-box solution
Enterprise tier requires custom sales negotiation

faqs.question: Is Saleor free?
faqs.answer: The open-source Saleor core is free and BSD-3-Clause licensed. A free Sandboxes tier on Saleor Cloud is also available for non-commercial prototyping and testing.

faqs.question: How much does Saleor Cloud cost?
faqs.answer: Select is $1,599/month (up to $200,000 GMV, 0.8% overage), Volume is $3,999/month (up to $1,000,000 GMV, 0.4% overage), and Enterprise is custom-priced with overage rates as low as 0.2%.

faqs.question: What license does Saleor use?
faqs.answer: Saleor's core is licensed under BSD-3-Clause.

faqs.question: Is Saleor headless?
faqs.answer: Yes, Saleor is a GraphQL-native, API-only headless commerce platform.

use_cases.title: Full-control headless commerce
use_cases.description: Build custom storefronts on top of a GraphQL-native API with no black-box layers.

use_cases.title: Compliance-sensitive commerce
use_cases.description: Run commerce infrastructure that meets SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, and PCI-DSS requirements.

use_cases.title: Omnichannel storefronts
use_cases.description: Serve web, mobile, retail, and agentic channels from a single API.

features: 0.8% fee on orders exceeding GMV limit
0.4% fee on orders exceeding GMV limit

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Saleor?
paragraphs: Saleor is an open-source, headless commerce platform built around a GraphQL-native API. The core is BSD-3-Clause licensed with 23.1k+ GitHub stars, and includes an extensible admin dashboard, 160+ webhooks, and OpenTelemetry-based observability.

Beyond the free open-source core and a free Sandboxes tier for prototyping, Saleor Cloud offers managed plans starting at $1,599/month for the Select tier, scaling to $3,999/month for Volume and custom Enterprise pricing, each with GMV-based limits and overage fees.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Saleor is for
paragraphs: Saleor targets commerce teams that want full control over a GraphQL-native, API-first backend and are equipped to build a custom storefront, rather than teams looking for an out-of-the-box store builder. Its compliance certifications also suit enterprises with strict security requirements.

enrichment.short_description: Vendure is an open-source, GPLv3 headless commerce platform built with TypeScript and GraphQL, with a paid Platform tier.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Vendure is a free, open-source (GPLv3) TypeScript/GraphQL commerce platform, with paid Platform and Cloud tiers.

features.title: B2B workflows
features.description: Quote-to-cash flows, bulk SKU entry, saved requisition lists, and repeat-order flows.

features.title: Advanced pricing
features.description: Customer-specific pricing, contract pricing, store credit, gift cards, and packaging-unit pricing.

features.title: Governance tools
features.description: SSO, audit trails, row-level permissions, and content versioning.

features.title: Search and discovery
features.description: Typesense-backed catalog search, a command palette, and wishlists.

features.title: Workflow engine
features.description: Custom entities and approval policies for complex ordering processes.

features.title: Enterprise storefront
features.description: A production-ready Next.js storefront with company accounts and multi-step checkout, included with Platform.

pros: Core is free forever and open source under GPLv3, with 8.2k+ GitHub stars
Built on a modern TypeScript, Node.js (NestJS), and GraphQL stack
Deep B2B feature set: quote-to-cash, approvals, contract pricing
Plugin-first architecture avoids forking the core
Used by known brands including IBM, Breitling, Swile, and Munch
cons: Platform tier pricing is not publicly disclosed and requires contacting sales
Vendure Cloud is not yet generally available (design-partner stage, GA targeted Q4 2026)
Developer-oriented setup requiring Node.js/TypeScript expertise

faqs.question: Is Vendure free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vendure Core is free forever and licensed under GPLv3.

faqs.question: What is Vendure Platform?
faqs.answer: Platform is the paid commercial tier, adding a production-ready Next.js storefront, 20+ enterprise plugins, AI tooling, dedicated support, and quarterly roadmap calls on top of Core.

faqs.question: Is Vendure Cloud available?
faqs.answer: Vendure Cloud is available now for design partners, with general availability targeted for Q4 2026.

faqs.question: What technology does Vendure use?
faqs.answer: Vendure is built with TypeScript, Node.js (NestJS), GraphQL, and React.

use_cases.title: Complex B2B commerce
use_cases.description: Run quote-to-cash and approval workflows for wholesale or enterprise buyers.

use_cases.title: TypeScript/GraphQL commerce backend
use_cases.description: Build a custom commerce backend using a familiar Node.js/GraphQL stack.

use_cases.title: Multi-channel enterprise commerce
use_cases.description: Run D2C, marketplace, and B2B commerce on one unified backend across multiple regions and currencies.

features: Full commerce framework and admin dashboard
Community support via Discord and GitHub

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Vendure?
paragraphs: Vendure is an open-source, headless commerce platform built with TypeScript, Node.js (NestJS), and GraphQL. Its Core edition is free forever under GPLv3 and includes a full commerce framework, admin dashboard, and plugin model, with 8.2k+ GitHub stars.

A paid Platform tier adds a production-ready Next.js storefront, enterprise plugins, AI tooling, and dedicated support, while Vendure Cloud — currently available to design partners ahead of a targeted Q4 2026 general availability — offers managed hosting for either Core or Platform projects.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Vendure is for
paragraphs: Vendure is built for developers and enterprises that need complex B2B commerce capabilities — quote-to-cash, contract pricing, approval workflows — on a modern TypeScript/GraphQL stack, without forking the core platform to add custom logic.
